Content / process description: Mixed-format fluid tasks including number/letter series, verbal analogies, odd-one-out, and short verbal logic problems. Items emphasize pattern discovery and relational integration with minimal cultural knowledge demands.
Approximate difficulty gradient: Items 1–4: single-rule series (e.g., constant difference, simple doubling), concrete analogies. Items 5–10: mixed-rule series (e.g., changing differences, nested patterns), more abstract analogies. Items 11–16: interleaved sequences, multi-step conditional reasoning, small logic puzzles.
Operational items / trials (N): 16 scored items (with age-based start points and basal/ceiling rules)
Intended index contribution: Primary contributor to Fluid Reasoning Index; moderate loading on Full Scale

Content / process description: Orally presented vocabulary items requiring definitions or explanations of words spanning concrete, everyday terms to abstract and academic vocabulary. Responses are scored with a 0–1–2 rubric for accuracy and richness.
Approximate difficulty gradient: Items 1–4: very high-frequency concrete words. Items 5–10: common academic and relational terms. Items 11–16: lower-frequency, more nuanced or abstract words appropriate for adult language samples.
Intended index contribution: Core contributor to Crystallized Knowledge Index; strong loading on Full Scale

Content / process description: Similarities items requiring abstracting a common concept between two words, plus general information items sampling broad, culture-fair knowledge in science, geography, and civics.
Approximate difficulty gradient: Early items: concrete similarities (e.g., everyday objects), basic facts taught in compulsory schooling. Later items: abstract conceptual similarities (e.g., rules vs. laws), less obvious but still widely taught information.
Intended index contribution: Secondary contributor to Crystallized Knowledge Index; supports verbal profile interpretation

Intended CHC coverage: Gwm (auditory short-term memory, working memory, attentional control)
Content / process description: Sequences of digits (and optionally letters) presented orally. Examinees repeat sequences forward, backward, and in ascending order; selected trials combine numbers and letters (letter–number sequencing). Trials increase in length until discontinue criteria are met.
Approximate difficulty gradient: Forward span lengths increase first (2→8 digits), followed by backward spans (2→7 digits), then sequencing/letter–number trials (3→8 elements) that place higher demands on mental manipulation and updating.
Operational items / trials (N): ~20 administered trials, of which ~12–16 contribute to scaled scores depending on basal/ceiling
Intended index contribution: Primary contributor to Working Memory Index; also supports interpretation of high‑level reasoning performance

Content / process description: Visually presented pattern-analysis tasks including pattern-matching, mental rotation judgments, and embedded-figure detection. Motor requirements are minimized by using pointing or simple drawing responses.
Approximate difficulty gradient: Items 1–4: same/different decisions on simple 2D shapes. Items 5–10: 2D and 3D rotation judgments with clearly different distractors. Items 11–16: more complex configurations and embedded targets requiring integration of multiple segments.
Intended index contribution: Primary contributor to Visual–Spatial Index; provides nonverbal estimate less dependent on language

Content / process description: Timed symbol search task in which examinees rapidly scan rows of simple symbols to decide whether any target symbol appears in each row. Performance is scored as number correct within a fixed time with penalties for errors.
Approximate difficulty gradient: Practice items ensure comprehension. Main form: early rows with few, highly distinct symbols; later rows with more symbols and greater visual similarity, increasing confusability but keeping operations simple and overlearned.
Development item pool (N): 3–4 full pages of rows (multiple parallel forms)
Operational items / trials (N): 1–2 timed forms (e.g., 90 seconds each), converted to a single scaled score
Intended index contribution: Primary contributor to Processing Speed Index; sensitive to age-related slowing and motor/visual factors

Content / process description: Timed digit–symbol substitution task using a key that pairs digits with simple symbols. Examinees draw the corresponding symbol under each digit as quickly and accurately as possible within the time limit.
Approximate difficulty gradient: Initial rows present a small set of digits with ample spacing; later rows present denser digit strings and more opportunities for confusion, while maintaining very low conceptual complexity.
Operational items / trials (N): 1 timed form (e.g., 120 seconds), producing a scaled score
Intended index contribution: Secondary contributor to Processing Speed Index; complements Symbol Scan by adding a motor output component
